2604.28031.title: Models Recall What They Violate: Constraint Adherence in Multi-Turn LLM Ideation
2604.28031.paper_summary: When researchers iteratively refine ideas with large language models, do the models preserve fidelity to the original objective? We introduce DriftBench, a benchmark for evaluating constraint adherence in multi-turn LLM-assisted scientific ideation. Across 2,146 scored benchmark runs spanning seven models from five providers (including two open-weight), four interaction conditions, and 38 research briefs from 24 scientific domains, we find that iterative pressure reliably increases structural complexity and often reduces adherence to original constraints. A restatement probe reveals a dissociation between declarative recall and behavioral adherence, as models accurately restate constraints they simultaneously violate. The knows-but-violates (KBV) rate, measuring constraint non-compliance despite preserved recall, ranges from 8% to 99% across models. Structured checkpointing partially reduces KBV rates but does not close the dissociation, and complexity inflation persists. Human validation against blind raters confirms that the LLM judge under-detects constraint violations, making reported constraint adherence scores conservative. Sensitivity analyses confirm the findings are robust to temperature (0.7 vs.\ 1.0) and pressure type (novelty vs.\ rigor). We release all briefs, prompts, rubrics, transcripts, and scores as an open benchmark.
2604.28031.paper_summary_zh: 當研究人員與大型語言模型反覆精煉想法時，這些模型是否保持對原始目標的忠實度？我們介紹 DriftBench，一個用於評估多輪 LLM 輔助科學構思中約束遵循的基準。在涵蓋來自五個提供者的七個模型（包括兩個開放權重）、四種互動條件以及來自 24 個科學領域的 38 個研究簡報的 2,146 次得分基準運行中，我們發現反覆施壓可靠地增加了結構複雜性，並且通常減少了對原始約束的遵循。一項重述探測顯示了陳述性回憶與行為遵循之間的脫節，因為模型能準確重述它們同時違反的約束。知道但違反（KBV）率，衡量儘管保留回憶卻不遵守約束的情況，在模型之間範圍從 8% 到 99%。結構化檢查點部分減少了 KBV 率，但並未消除脫節，且複雜性膨脹仍然存在。與盲評者的人類驗證確認 LLM 評判者未能充分檢測約束違反，使得報告的約束遵循分數顯得保守。敏感性分析確認這些發現對溫度（0.7 與 1.0）和壓力類型（新穎性與嚴謹性）是穩健的。我們將所有簡報、提示、評分標準、逐字稿和分數作為開放基準釋出。
2604.28031.author: Garvin Kruthof et.al.
